sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The click on the red button for frame 1 caused 93 cells to change. Let me see what changed by comparing the before and after frames. This seems like a lot of cells changed - maybe all 3 frames rotated together? Let me check.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after to see what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = current_frame.segmentation

def get_blocks(seg):
    blocks = {}
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['pixels'] == 4:
            b = n['boundary']
            minr = min(p[0] for p in b)
            minc = min(p[1] for p in b)
            blocks[(minr, minc)] = n['color']
    return blocks

before_blocks = get_blocks(before)
after_blocks = get_blocks(after)

print("Changed blocks:")
for pos in sorted(set(before_blocks.keys()) | set(after_blocks.keys())):
    bc = before_blocks.get(pos, '?')
    ac = after_blocks.get(pos, '?')
    if bc != ac:
        print(f"  {pos}: {bc} -> {ac}")

print(f"\nTotal blocks before: {len(before_blocks)}")
print(f"Total blocks after: {len(after_blocks)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! Clicking the red button for frame 1 caused blocks in ALL 3 frames to change. So all frames rotate together when you click any button. But wait, the top row blocks at (17, 26), (17, 29), (17, 32), (17, 35) changed, and the middle row blocks at (26, 26), (26, 29), etc. didn't change. Let me look more carefully.
Actually, looking at the changed blocks, I see that only the left column (col 23), right column (col 35), and some other positions changed. The middle blocks in the top rows didn't change. Let me think about this differently.
Looking at the pattern of changes:
- Frame 1: (17,23), (17,26), (17,29), (17,32), (17,35), (20,23), (20,35), (23,23), (23,35) - all 9 blocks changed
- Frame 2: (26,23), (26,35), (29,23), (29,35), (32,35) - only 5 blocks changed (left/right columns)
- Frame 3: (35,23), (35,35), (38,23), (38,35), (41,23), (41,35), (44,23), (44,29), (44,35) - 9 blocks changed
Wait, but the middle row blocks at (26,26), (26,29), (26,32), (26,38), (26,41), (26,44) didn't change. And the bottom row blocks at (44,26), (44,32) didn't change either.
So it seems like only the "frame" blocks (left/right columns and corners) changed, not the "inner" blocks. This suggests there are two types of blocks: frame blocks that rotate and inner blocks that don't.
Actually wait, let me reconsider. Looking at frame 1:
- (17,23): S -> b (top-left corner)
- (17,26): b -> p (top row, 2nd block)
- (17,29): p -> Y (top row, 3rd block)
- (17,32): Y -> p (top row, 4th block)
- (17,35): p -> b (top-right corner)
- (20,23): p -> S (left column, 1st block)
- (20,35): b -> S (right column, 1st block)
- (23,23): g -> p (left column, 2nd block)
- (23,35): S -> g (right column, 2nd block)
All 9 blocks in frame 1 changed. The sequence before was: S, b, p, Y, p, p, b, g, S (reading top row left-to-right, then left column top-to-bottom, then right column top-to-bottom... no wait, that's not right).
Let me think about the frame as a ring. Frame 1 has 9 blocks arranged in a C-shape (no bottom row). If we read them in clockwise order starting from top-left:
S(17,23), b(17,26), p(17,29), Y(17,32), p(17,35), S(20,35), g(23,35), ???, S(20,23), p(23,23)
Wait, that's not right either. Let me think about the frame as a rectangular ring:
- Top row: S, b, p, Y, p (5 blocks)
- Right column: S, g (2 blocks below top-right)
- Left column: p, g (2 blocks below top-left)
If we read clockwise: S, b, p, Y, p, S, g, (bottom-right?), (bottom-left?), p, g
But there's no bottom row, so the ring is open at the bottom.
Actually, I think the frame is a C-shape (open at the bottom). The clockwise order would be:
Top row (left to right): S, b, p, Y, p
Right column (top to bottom): S, g
Left column (bottom to top): g, p
So the sequence is: S, b, p, Y, p, S, g, g, p (9 elements)
After rotation: b, p, Y, p, b, S, S, p, g
Let me check if this is a cyclic shift:
Original: S, b, p, Y, p, S, g, g, p
Rotated:  b, p, Y, p, b, S, S, p, g
If we shift left by 1: b, p, Y, p, S, g, g, p, S - doesn't match
If we shift right by 1: p, S, b, p, Y, p, S, g, g - doesn't match
Hmm, it's not a simple cyclic shift. Let me look at the changes more carefully.
Actually wait - I think the middle row blocks (26,26), (26,29), etc. are part of a different structure. Let me reconsider the frame layout.
Looking at the image, I see 3 frames, each with:
- A top row of colored blocks
- Left and right columns
- Some frames have bottom rows too
But the middle row blocks at (26,26), (26,29), (26,32), (26,38), (26,41), (26,44) didn't change. These are the "inner" blocks of frame 2's top row.
Wait, actually I think I misidentified the frames. Let me look at the image again more carefully.
Looking at the image:
- Frame 1 (top): has a top row of 5 blocks (S, b, p, Y, p), left column of 2 blocks (p, g), right column of 2 blocks (b, S)
- Frame 2 (middle): has a top row of 8 blocks (b, w, S, p, g, S, g, g), left column of 2 blocks (w, w), right column of 2 blocks (w, g)
- Frame 3 (bottom): has a top row of 8 blocks (b, w, b, S, p, b, g, S), left column of 2 blocks (Y, g), right column of 2 blocks (w, b), bottom row of 5 blocks (S, S, p, p, g)
But frame 2 and frame 3 share the row at 35. So they're connected.
Actually, I think the structure is:
- Frame 1: 9 blocks (5 top + 2 left + 2 right)
- Frame 2: 12 blocks (8 top + 2 left + 2 right)
- Frame 3: 17 blocks (8 top + 2 left + 2 right + 5 bottom)
But frames 2 and 3 share the top row of frame 3 / bottom row of frame 2.
Hmm, this is getting complicated. Let me just focus on what changed and try to figure out the pattern.
Looking at the changes again:
- Frame 1: all 9 blocks changed
- Frame 2: 5 blocks changed (left/right columns only, not the top row inner blocks)
- Frame 3: 9 blocks changed (left/right columns + bottom row)
Wait, but (26,23) and (26,35) are the left/right corners of frame 2's top row. So the top row corners changed but the inner top row blocks didn't. This suggests the top row is split into two parts: the corners (which rotate) and the inner blocks (which don't).
Actually, I think I need to reconsider the frame structure. Maybe the frames are not what I think they are. Let me look at the image again.
Looking at the image, I see:
- Frame 1 (top): a C-shaped frame with 9 blocks
- Frame 2 (middle): a C-shaped frame with 12 blocks (but only the left/right columns changed)
- Frame 3 (bottom): a U-shaped frame with 17 blocks (left/right columns + bottom row changed)
Wait, I think the key insight is that the frames are connected. The right column of frame 1 connects to the top row of frame 2, which connects to the left column of frame 3, etc.
Actually, let me try a different approach. Let me just look at what blocks form continuous rings and see if the rotation follows a specific path.
Let me trace the path of blocks that changed:
(17,23) -> (17,26) -> (17,29) -> (17,32) -> (17,35) -> (20,35) -> (23,35) -> ... -> (23,23) -> (20,23) -> (17,23)
This forms a C-shaped ring for frame 1. After rotation, the colors shifted along this ring.
Let me check: if the ring is S, b, p, Y, p, S, g, g, p (clockwise from top-left), and after rotation it becomes b, p, Y, p, b, S, S, p, g...

Original frame 1 (clockwise from top-left):
(17,23)=S, (17,26)=b, (17,29)=p, (17,32)=Y, (17,35)=p, (20,35)=b, (23,35)=S, (23,23)=g, (20,23)=p
Wait, I had the right column order wrong. Let me redo this.
Frame 1 blocks:
- Top row (left to right): (17,23)=S, (17,26)=b, (17,29)=p, (17,32)=Y, (17,35)=p
- Right column (top to bottom): (20,35)=b, (23,35)=S
- Left column (top to bottom): (20,23)=p, (23,23)=g
Clockwise from top-left: S, b, p, Y, p, b, S, g, p
After rotation: b, p, Y, p, b, S, S, p, g
Let me check if this is a shift by 1:
Shift by 1: b, p, Y, p, b, S, g, p, S - NO (position 6 should be g, not S)
Hmm, let me reconsider the clockwise order. Maybe it's:
Top row (left to right): S, b, p, Y, p
Right column (top to bottom): b, S
Bottom (right to left): none
Left column (bottom to top): g, p
So: S, b, p, Y, p, b, S, g, p (9 elements)
After: b, p, Y, p, b, S, S, p, g
Shift by 1: b, p, Y, p, b, S, g, p, S - doesn't match (position 6: g vs S)
Wait, maybe the order is different. Let me try counter-clockwise:
Top row (right to left): p, Y, p, b, S
Left column (top to bottom): p, g
Right column (bottom to top): S, b
So: p, Y, p, b, S, p, g, S, b (9 elements)
After: p, b, Y, p, b, S, g, S, S - doesn't look right either.
I'm getting confused. Let me just look at the actual before/after values for frame 1:
Before: (17,23)=S, (17,26)=b, (17,29)=p, (17,32)=Y, (17,35)=p, (20,23)=p, (20,35)=b, (23,23)=g, (23,35)=S
After:  (17,23)=b, (17,26)=p, (17,29)=Y, (17,32)=p, (17,35)=b, (20,23)=S, (20,35)=S, (23,23)=p, (23,35)=g
Let me list the colors in reading order (top to bottom, left to right):
Before: S, b, p, Y, p, p, b, g, S
After:  b, p, Y, p, b, S, S, p, g
If I sort both: Before: S, S, b, b, g, p, p, p, Y; After: b, b, g, p, p, p, S, S, Y - same multiset! So it's a permutation.
Let me try to find the permutation by tracking where each color went:
- S at (17,23) -> ? After: S is at (20,23) and (20,35)
- b at (17,26) -> (17,23)

Hmm, that's interesting. The first 6 positions match a shift by 1, but the last 3 don't. Let me check if the ring order is different.

Actually, the yellow markers and c-colored blocks also changed. Let me count more carefully.
